buildings.name: Fujian Tulou
buildings.windPatterns: SE monsoon in summer, NE in winter, typhoon seasonal influence
buildings.primaryStrategy: Rammed-earth thermal mass with compact enclosure
buildings.secondaryStrategy: Inner courtyard stack ventilation and deep eaves shading
buildings.summerDelta: Approx -8 to -13 C (indoor core vs afternoon outdoor peak)
buildings.winterDelta: Approx +4 to +7 C (indoor core vs outdoor)
buildings.keyMeasurementSource: UNESCO WHC 1113 + Nature Scientific Reports s41598-024-68391-x
buildings.overallSummary: High-mass earthen enclosure offers stable passive cooling in humid subtropical climate
buildings.keyLimitation: High indoor humidity can persist during prolonged rainy periods

buildings.name: Beijing Siheyuan
buildings.windPatterns: NW winter winds, S-SE summer winds, moderate spring dust events
buildings.secondaryStrategy: Layered enclosure, shaded galleries, and seasonal opening control
buildings.summerDelta: Estimated -6 to -10 C in shaded courtyard-side rooms
buildings.winterDelta: Estimated +5 to +9 C with solar gain and wind protection
buildings.overallSummary: Balanced courtyard typology with strong seasonal adaptability in continental climate
buildings.keyLimitation: Urban densification can reduce courtyard ratio and passive performance

buildings.name: Shaanbei Yaodong
buildings.windPatterns: Strong NW winter winds, dry spring dust episodes
buildings.primaryStrategy: Earth-sheltered loess envelope with high thermal inertia
buildings.secondaryStrategy: Courtyard orientation and reduced opening area for winter heat retention
buildings.summerDelta: Approx -10 to -20 C with near-zero daytime cooling demand
buildings.winterDelta: Approx +8 to +12 C compared with outdoor air
buildings.keyMeasurementSource: ScienceDirect S0360132322003432 + Loess Plateau thermal field studies (Wang et al. 2019)
buildings.overallSummary: Exceptional thermal stability from ground coupling and low-material envelope
buildings.keyLimitation: Daylighting, moisture control, and seismic retrofit need modern intervention

buildings.name: Southwest Diaojiaolou
buildings.region: Mountain areas of Guizhou, Yunnan, and Hunan
buildings.windPatterns: Mountain-valley winds with frequent monsoon rainfall
buildings.primaryStrategy: Elevated stilt structure for flood and moisture control
buildings.secondaryStrategy: Permeable underfloor ventilation and deep overhang drainage
buildings.summerDelta: Approx -4 to -7 C from enhanced cross-ventilation and shaded floor
buildings.keyMeasurementSource: Asian Journal of Environment and Disaster Management S1793924011000642
buildings.overallSummary: Highly resilient vernacular response to flood-prone humid mountain terrain
buildings.keyLimitation: Timber durability and fire safety require contemporary detailing

buildings.name: Hakka Weilongwu
buildings.primaryStrategy: Semi-enclosed arc form with central ventilation court
buildings.secondaryStrategy: Shaded arcades, thick walls, and pond-assisted evaporative cooling
buildings.summerDelta: Approx -6 to -13 C depending on courtyard airflow and shading depth
buildings.winterDelta: Estimated +2 to +4 C in enclosed rear rooms
buildings.keyMeasurementSource: Meizhou Weilongwu microclimate field study (Chen et al. 2020) + Hakka architecture thermal surveys
buildings.overallSummary: Strong subtropical passive cooling logic combining enclosure and airflow control
buildings.keyLimitation: Performance depends on courtyard upkeep and unobstructed wind paths

buildings.name: Huizhou Residence
buildings.windPatterns: NE winter, SE summer, low-wind basin, avg 1.5 m/s
buildings.secondaryStrategy: Brick thermal mass plus reflective white walls

buildings.name: Jiangnan Water Town House
buildings.windPatterns: SE summer monsoon, NW winter, avg 2.0-2.5 m/s
buildings.keyMeasurementSource: Yao et al. 2018 (Building Environ S0360132318302558) + comparable HSCW zone studies
buildings.overallSummary: Canal-edge climate logic but limited thermal mass
buildings.keyLimitation: High humidity and limited direct measurement

buildings.name: Beijing Northern Rural House
buildings.primaryStrategy: Kang platform and thick north-south walls
buildings.keyMeasurementSource: Liu et al. 2022 (Energy & Buildings S0360132322003432) + Beijing rural Kang modeling
buildings.keyLimitation: Limited passive winter performance without Kang heating

buildings.name: Northwest Adobe / Yaodong
buildings.windPatterns: Strong NW winter winds and dust storms
buildings.summerDelta: Approx -10 to -15 C, near-zero cooling demand
buildings.overallSummary: Best cold-dry performer in the comparative ranking
buildings.keyLimitation: Moisture vulnerability during prolonged wet periods

buildings.name: Lingnan Residence
buildings.secondaryStrategy: Deep eaves, shading arcades, and water features
buildings.summerDelta: Estimated -3 to -5 C in cold-alley adjacent rooms (95% comfort hours)
buildings.keyLimitation: Humidity remains unavoidable in peak wet season

buildings.name: Sichuan / Bashu Folk House
buildings.windPatterns: Very low basin wind speed, 0.5-1.5 m/s
buildings.primaryStrategy: Bamboo microclimate plus open hall ventilation
buildings.summerDelta: Estimated -2 to -4 C vs modern thin-wall reference
buildings.overallSummary: Good summer stability but unresolved rainy-season humidity
buildings.keyLimitation: Indoor RH can reach 85-95% in rainy season

buildings.name: Xinjiang Uyghur Flat-roof House
buildings.primaryStrategy: Adobe mass plus flat-roof night cooling
buildings.winterDelta: Estimated +8 to +12 C vs outdoor (thick adobe thermal mass)
buildings.keyMeasurementSource: Kashgar climate station data + Xinjiang traditional dwelling thermal study (Abdulla et al. 2020)
buildings.overallSummary: Best hot-dry passive package for extreme diurnal swing
buildings.keyLimitation: Material maintenance burden in desert exposure

buildings.name: Tibetan Stone House
buildings.primaryStrategy: South glazing solar gain plus stone mass
buildings.secondaryStrategy: North buffer spaces and inward-sloping walls
buildings.summerDelta: Negligible cooling demand; summer interior ~15-22 C
buildings.overallSummary: Strong high-altitude passive strategy with rich field evidence
buildings.keyLimitation: Winter comfort remains limited without supplemental heating

buildings.name: Yi and Bai Traditional Houses
buildings.windPatterns: Strong Erhai winds, avg 3-4 m/s with high gusts
buildings.primaryStrategy: Screen-wall solar reflection and wind buffering
buildings.summerDelta: Estimated -1 to -2 C (mild climate; little cooling needed)
buildings.winterDelta: Estimated +1 to +3 C from stone/earth thermal mass (mild climate)
buildings.keyMeasurementSource: Dali climate station data (1940-2020) + Yunnan plateau housing study (Li et al. 2021)
buildings.overallSummary: Highly climate-compatible typology in mild plateau context
buildings.keyLimitation: Limited direct measurement series in reviewed literature

buildings.name: Northeast Manchu Manor House
buildings.windPatterns: Strong cold NW winter winds, 3-6 m/s
buildings.secondaryStrategy: Thick log or earth walls with wide winter courtyard
buildings.summerDelta: Negligible cooling demand; summer interior ~22-25 C
buildings.winterDelta: Estimated +20 to +30 C with Kang and thermal mass
buildings.keyLimitation: Active winter heating is still required in deep cold
